title: First Authorised Scan
learning_objectives: Perform authorised active reconnaissance within a defined scope
Use Nmap for host discovery and service enumeration
scenario: Following your passive recon report, the NGO has authorised limited active reconnaissance against their staging server in a lab environment. Your scope is defined: identify live hosts, open ports, and running services. Stay within scope at all times.

scenario_context.setting: NGO staging server in controlled lab environment
scenario_context.challenge: Active scanning within strict scope boundaries
scenario_context.relevance: Scope compliance is non-negotiable in professional penetration testing. African organisations increasingly require formal authorisation documentation.

subtasks.title: Authorisation & Scope Documentation
subtasks.description: Document your authorisation scope: target IPs, permitted techniques, time window, and emergency contacts.
subtasks.detailed_guidance: Use the provided template. Fill in every field. This document protects you legally. Include: authorised IP ranges, permitted scan types, time window, out-of-scope systems, escalation contacts.
subtasks.evidence_required: Completed scope document using the provided template

subtasks.title: Host Discovery
subtasks.description: Perform ping sweeps to identify live hosts on the target network segment.
subtasks.detailed_guidance: Use nmap -sn to discover live hosts. Try different discovery methods if ICMP is blocked. Document every command used and its output.
subtasks.evidence_required: Host discovery output with live hosts identified

subtasks.title: Port Scanning & Service Detection
subtasks.description: Scan discovered hosts for open ports and identify running services with version information.
subtasks.detailed_guidance: Use nmap -sV -sC for service detection. Consider different scan types: SYN scan, connect scan. Save output in multiple formats (-oN, -oX). Note any unexpected services.
subtasks.evidence_required: Scan output showing open ports, services, and versions

subtasks.title: Active Recon Report
subtasks.description: Compile a brief professional report covering methodology, scope compliance, and all findings.
subtasks.detailed_guidance: Report structure: Scope Confirmation, Methodology, Host Discovery Results, Service Enumeration Results, Initial Observations, Recommendations for Next Phase.
subtasks.evidence_required: Active recon report with scan evidence attached

reflection_prompt: During your scan, you accidentally discover a host outside your authorised scope responding to your packets. What do you do? Walk through every decision point.

quiz.question: The most important step before active scanning is:
options: Getting written authorisation and defining scope
quiz.explanation: Without written authorisation, any scanning could be illegal regardless of intent.

quiz.question: nmap -sn performs:
quiz.explanation: The -sn flag tells Nmap to perform host discovery only, without scanning ports.

mentor_notes: Scope compliance is the most critical element. A technically perfect scan that goes out of scope should fail. Check that the student documented authorisation before scanning.
